channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gematsu.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gematsu.com

item.title: Gecko Gods coming to Switch 2 on June 25 alongside ‘Forgotten Cove’ update
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Switch
  • Switch 2

A whole new island for players to explore.

Publisher Super Rare Originals and developer Inresin will release a Switch 2 version of puzzle platformer Gecko Gods on June 25 alongside the “Forgotten Cove” update, which will add a whole new island, the companies announced. Users who own the Switch version will be able to upgrade to the Switch 2 version at no additional cost.

Gecko Gods first launched for PlayStation 5, Switch, and PC via Steam on April 16

Here is an overview of the game, via Super Rare Originals:

About

Gecko Gods lets you take control of a curious and adorable gecko where you will climb across sunlit ruins, weave your way through lush caves, and uncover the remnants of a lost civilization. Every path you take invites discovery as you glide between islands on your gecko-sized boat, each with their own secrets to uncover.

Key Features

  • Explore Freely – Scale walls, ceilings and cliffs with the gecko’s effortless agility. Climb to the highest peaks or the deepest caves as you play with near unlimited freedom of movement.
  • A Gecko-Sized Journey – Experience a world designed for curiosity and gentle adventure, where movement itself is part of the joy. Enjoy the pitter-patter of the geckos little feet on the sand or the gentle sound of the waves as you sail through the ocean.
  • Solve Ancient Puzzles – Delve into ruins and temples filled with intuitive, environmental challenges. Solve puzzles to unlock the secrets of each island, and get hints from the critters you meet along the way.
  • Sail Between Islands – Drift across tranquil seas to discover unique landscapes, each with its own atmosphere and hidden life.


https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/gecko-gods-coming-to-switch-2-on-june-25-alongside-forgotten-cove-update
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: My Hero Academia: All’s Justice coming to Switch 2 on September 3 in Japan, September 4 worldwide
item.content:encoded:
• Switch 2

Due out alongside free "Mini Game" content.

Publisher Bandai Namco and developer Byking will release My Hero Academia: All’s Justice for Switch 2 on September 3 in Japan and September 4 worldwide, the companies announced. The physical edition will ship on a game-key card.

My Hero Academia: All’s Justice first launched for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC via Steam on February 6.

The additional content “Mini Game (All 10 Types)” will launch for free for all versions of the game alongside the Switch 2 version’s launch.

Here is an overview of the game, via Bandai Namco:

About

SMASH through My Hero Academia‘s final story arc and triumph over your foes in spectacular 3v3 battles! Follow Deku and the journeys of other characters in the Final War between Heroes and Villains, and experience the world-shaking, climactic clash between One For All and All For One in My Hero Academia: All’s Justice!

My Hero Academia: All’s Justice is the super-powered 3D arena fighter where your favorite characters from the My Hero Academia franchise appear in their latest and most powerful forms, framed within a Refined Battle System. Furthermore, dive into a variety of game modes, including original scenario based Team-Up Mission where you’ll experience hero life as a U.A. High 1-A student in a Virtual Space!

Relive the Final War Storyline

Experience the final battle of My Hero Academia from a new perspective in My Hero Academia: All’s Justice‘s story mode. Re-live Bakugo’s fearless stand against All For One, experience Deku’s heroic struggle to bring justice and redemption to his fated foe, and more in a unique and cinematic take on the climactic clash of My Hero Academia.

The Largest My Hero Academia Roster

Heroes and Villains collide in the gigantic roster of My Hero Academia characters! Play your favorite U.A. Students, including all of Class 1-A, Pro Heroes like Mirko and Best Jeanist, and Villains such as All For One, Shigaraki and Dabi from across the full story of My Hero Academia, including their final and full-powered versions!

Unleash Your Quirks and Potential in Fast-paced Strategic Battles

Take control of your favorite U.A. Students, Pro Heroes, and Villains, and utilize their unique Quirks to pummel, blast, and trick your foes.

Unleash each character’s “Quirk” like never before by activating “Rising,” which allows attack power, movement speed, and Quirk actions all enhanced.

Mix and match your team’s Quirks to find synergies and add depth to your tactics and strategies. Chain combos while switching characters to seize victory!

A Day in the Life of a Hero

Step into the shoes of Deku and Class 1-A students in “Team-Up Mission” and tackle training exercise in a Virtual Space! Based on an original game scenario form a team with Class 1-A students as your allies and complete various missions and triumph over Villains in an exclusive map!

Furthermore, jump into “Archives Battle” which will be unlocked by completing Team-Up Mission to experience iconic battles from across the full story of My Hero Academia.

Watch a new trailer below.

Switch 2 Announce Trailer

English

Japanese



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/my-hero-academia-alls-justice-coming-to-switch-2-on-september-3-in-japan-september-4-worldwide
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Genshin Impact Version Luna VIII update ‘Song of the Welkin Moon: Scherzo – Sunny Summer Fontinalia’ launches July 1
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Smartphone
  • Xbox Series

Reunite with Sandrone and embark on an adventure to the moon!

The Version Luna VIII update for Genshin Impact, dubbed “Song of the Welkin Moon: Scherzo – Sunny Summer Fontinalia,” will launch on July 1, developer miHoYo announced.

Here is an overview of the update, via miHoYo:

In this update, players will reunite with Sandrone, who makes an unexpected return after sacrificing herself in the fierce battle of Nod-Krai. Meanwhile, beyond the skies of Teyvat, the Frost Moon will become available as a permanent new area, inviting players to explore its vast and breathtaking landscapes.

Sandrone, the Seventh of the Fatui Harbingers, makes an unexpected return in this version as a 5-star Cryo Claymore wielder. After a tense first encounter, players witnessed her selfless sacrifice in the fierce battle against The Doctor, helping Columbina and the Traveler secure victory. Now that she has returned, the truth behind her resurrection is set to be revealed.

Arriving alongside Sandrone is Stellar-Conduct, a brand-new Cryo-Electro reaction. When triggered by certain Cryo and Electro characters, the surrounding area will briefly transform into a Polestar Field. This field can store excess Cryo and Electro energy and convert it into buffs, increasing the Stellar-Conduct DMG, as well as Cryo DMG and Electro DMG dealt by all characters within its range, while also reducing opponents’ Physical RES. Certain existing Cryo and Electro characters can also receive unique enhancements while within the field.

In combat, Sandrone can increase the entire party’s Stellar-Conduct DMG through the Polestar Field. She commands Fagio, a fully autonomous construct that deals Stellar-Conduct DMG with each Charged Attack and accumulates Decoding Power. Once Fagio reaches its firing limit, its attack intervals will increase. By using her Elemental Skill, Sandrone can maneuver through the Polestar Field while firing at opponents, reducing Decoding Power to prevent Fagio from overloading. Her Elemental Burst bombards opponents with artillery fire, and beam DMG dealt within the Polestar Field will be considered Stellar-Conduct DMG.

  • ❮
  • ❯

Sandrone will make her debut in the first half of the Version Luna VIII Character Event Wishes alongside Citlali’s rerun, while the second half will feature the return of Columbina and Raiden Shogun.

Players will board a spacecraft and journey beyond the skies of Teyvat, finally setting foot on the moon, where a vast new region awaits exploration. The moon is home to many breathtaking sights unlike anything seen in Teyvat, including a luminous yet treacherous lunar sea and a space station suspended in outer space, from which players can gaze upon Teyvat as it quietly rotates in the distance. In addition, majestic ancient ruins await exploration, revealing more hidden stories tied to the ancient dragon race. A mysterious ancient power permeates the lunar surface, allowing players to traverse various types of terrain and manipulate the local gravity. To further improve the exploration experience, both the moon and Teyvat maps will receive a new layered display feature that shows the player’s current level in real time.

The Fontinalia Film Festival sets sail aboard the Wingalet in Fontaine, where Charlotte, Citlali, and many familiar faces gather to celebrate a midsummer water festival. Sandrone will also come aboard for the journey, while Columbina arrives with the film crew for a shoot. The event story will become permanent. Players can help manage the ship’s aquarium and attract visitors to join the festivities. After completing the event, players can invite Charlotte to their party for free and obtain a brand-new outfit for her. In addition, Citlali’s new outfit will be available at a limited-time discount. Players can also take part in a dance challenge back in Natlan, explore new pieces in the Repertoire of Myriad Melodies, and try out new cards in Genius Invokation TCG.

In this update, Miliastra Wonderland will receive a brand-new summer-themed lobby and summer-themed outfits. By completing fun activities, players can also claim free rewards, including Prismatic Crystals and an elegant 4-star set. For Craftspeople, the Editor will receive several practical upgrades, along with a selection of new environment assets that open up more possibilities for interactive gameplay and diverse visual design.

Genshin Impact is available now for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, PC via client and Epic Games Store, iOS via the App Store, and Android via Google Play.

Watch a new set of trailers below. View a new set of screenshots at the gallery.

Version Luna VIII Update: “Song of the Welkin Moon: Scherzo – Sunny Summer Fontinalia” Trailer

English

Japanese

Korean

Simplified Chinese

Snezhnaya Ecology Teaser: “Laws of the Bitter Frost”

item.title: Grand Theft Auto VI pre-orders open June 25, cover art revealed
item.content:encoded:
• PS5
  • Xbox Series

Due out on November 19.

Pre-orders for Grand Theft Auto VI will open on June 25 on digital storefronts and at other select retailers, Rockstar Games announced.

Grand Theft Auto VI will launch for PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series on November 19.

Watch the cover art reveal trailer below. Or view the cover art at the gallery.

Cover Art Reveal Trailer

Cover Art

  • ❮
  • ❯


https://www.gematsu.com/2026/06/grand-theft-auto-vi-pre-orders-open-june-25-cover-art-revealed
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Famitsu Sales: 6/8/26 – 6/14/26
item.content:encoded:
• PS5
  • Switch
  • Switch 2
  • Xbox Series

Powerful Pro Baseball 2026-2027 opens at 100,976 retail copies sold.

Famitsu has published its estimated physical game software data for Japan for the week of June 8, 2026 to June 25, 2026.

Powerful Pro Baseball 2026-2027 for Switch was the highest selling new and overall release this week, opening at 100,976 retail copies sold. A digital-only PlayStation 4 version was also released.

On the hardware side, Switch 2 sold 25,793 units, the PlayStation 5 family sold 8,184 units, the Switch family sold 4,933 units, and the Xbox Series family sold 572 units.

Get the full sales charts below.

Software Sales (followed by lifetime sales)

  1. [NSW] Powerful Pro Baseball 2026-2027 (Konami, 06/11/26) – 100,976 (New)
  2. [NSW] Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream (Nintendo, 04/16/26) – 37,888 (1,347,070)
  3. [SW2] eFootball Kick-Off! (Konami, 06/03/26) – 8,143 (28,190)
  4. [SW2] Pokemon Pokopia (The Pokemon Company, 03/05/26) – 7,781 (1,056,982)
  5. [PS5] ASTRO BOT (SIE, 09/06/24) – 6,533 (111,250)
  6. [SW2] Mario Kart World (Nintendo, 06/05/25) – 5,042 (2,970,496)
  7. [SW2] Yoshi and the Mysterious Book (Nintendo, 05/21/26) – 4,249 (64,802)
  8. [SW2] Final Fantasy VII Rebirth (Square Enix, 06/03/26) – 4,169 (34,826)
  9. [SW2] Inazuma Eleven: Victory Road (LEVEL-5, 06/11/26) – 3,544 (New)
  10. [NSW] Minecraft (Microsoft, 06/21/18) – 3,379 (4,222,322)

Rankings 11 to 30 will be announced on June 19.

  1. [PS5] 007 First Light (IO Interactive, 05/27/26) –
  2. [SW2] A-Train9 Evolution (ARTDINK, 06/04/26) –
  3. [PS5] Ghost of Yotei (SIE, 10/02/25) –
  4. [NSW] Animal Crossing: New Horizons (Nintendo, 03/20/20) –
  5. [NSW] Super Smash Bros. Ultimate (Nintendo, 12/07/18) –
  6. [NSW] Splatoon 3 (Nintendo, 09/09/22) –
  7. [NSW] Nintendo Switch Sports (Nintendo, 04/29/22) –
  8. [NSW] Super Mario Galaxy + Super Mario Galaxy 2 (Nintendo, 10/02/25) –
  9. [SW2] Super Mario Party Jamboree – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition + Jamboree TV (Nintendo, 07/24/25) –
  10. [NSW] Super Mario Party Jamboree (Nintendo, 10/17/24) –
  11. [NSW] Mario Kart 8 Deluxe (Nintendo, 04/28/17) –
  12. [SW2] Animal Crossing: New Horizons – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition (Nintendo, 01/15/26) –
  13. [NSW] It Takes Two (12/08/22) –
  14. [PS5] PRAGMATA (Capcom, 04/17/26) –
  15. [SW2] Momotaro Dentetsu 2: Anata no Machi mo Kitto Aru – Higashi Nihon Hen + Nishi Nihon Hen – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition (Konami, 11/13/25) –
  16. [SW2] Final Fantasy VII Remake Intergrade (Square Enix, 01/22/26) –
  17. [NSW] Clubhouse Games: 51 Worldwide Classics (Nintendo, 06/05/20) –
  18. [SW2] PRAGMATA (Capcom, 04/24/26) –
  19. [SW2] Super Mario Bros. Wonder – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition + Meetup in Bellabel Park (Nintendo, 03/26/26) –
  20. [SW2] Tales of Arise: Beyond the Dawn Edition (Bandai Namco, 05/21/26) –
  21. [NSW] Momotaro Dentetsu 2: Anata no Machi mo Kitto Aru – Higashi Nihon Hen + Nishi Nihon Hen (Konami, 11/13/25) –
  22. [SW2] The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition (06/05/25) –

Hardware Sales (followed by lifetime sales)

  1. Switch 2 – 25,793 (5,914,065)
  2. PlayStation 5 Digital Edition – 6,610 (1,316,161)
  3. Switch Lite – 2,445 (6,979,497)
  4. Switch OLED Model – 1,831 (9,590,639)
  5. PlayStation 5 Pro – 1,257 (363,209)
  6. Switch – 657 (20,301,736)
  7. Xbox Series X Digital Edition – 326 (32,238)
  8. PlayStation 5 – 317 (5,9240,422)
  9. Xbox Series X – 129 (327,620)
  10. Xbox Series S – 117 (342,445)
